author: Matt
msg: I think we are all here trying to succeed. There are varying visions of what that success looks like, and even more perspectives on how it would be best achieved. People building small farms or large farms put in work too. Some put it in because the split limits were  coming and that didn't necessarily line up with what there plan with this was. Either way... does not matter. We all have time, effort, and money in this. Those three elements vary hugely amongst us but may be relatively equal depending on each person's situation.  No one way is the "correct" way. What works for one may not work for another. Let's all just support each other's efforts/goals. We will get there.

author: Jouni Kuisma
msg:
Hi,
People are talking here about number of phones on same IP and speculating whether that makes the rewards drop. I think the reason is not the number of phones (sure, if you have over 20 it might be). Imo the rewards seem to depend on something else. Has anyone thought it might have something to do with time or dates? I am asking because to me it seems roughly that all my leases which were made on the early stages (like from November to January) are working well and the latter ones (beginning from Feb) - whether they are my own or someone elses - are working poorly. Might be a coincidence but could it be? What do you think? Has anyone else detected similar observations?

author: ✌🏽🥸Shazza YNWA
msg:
I’d rather just switch off tbh until we know it’s all stable it’s got to that point for me, I feel me and my ULOs have been very invested with our time trying to support the project.

I think this last issue should have been combined with a total reset as the rewards would have been low anyway, but to continue facing issues daily is the problem, losing ULOs is a problem, investing in sims and routers and going above and beyond is now a problem…..how much can one keep fighting when things are going from bad to worse…..I spent time activating all my sims for the sole purpose maybe it would help receiving calls for that phase, not a sausage….no missed calls or anything….and now we moving on to sms messaging, then that will pass…..all the above and more would be bearable if the rewards were as they were, but now…everyone is seeing 0.005 or less and some lucky ones seeing an increase….what are we to make of that?! Bob Hope no hope as we say here in the UK….

author: Sojourner
msg:
It’s clear that phones on WiFi only are the most affected. What I don’t understand is my mixed phones (sims that are connected to WiFi when at home, approximately 50% of the day) are no better earners than WiFi only phones. It’s as if any of those phones connect to my home internet even for a few minutes get rewards slashed to nothing even though throughout the day they were on sim. 

I am at a point where I need to decide to eliminate my small WiFi only phone farm of just 7 phones in order to reap a larger benefit from the sim phones I have connected at home now.

author: ✌🏽🥸Shazza YNWA
msg: Yes, I will be doing this for sure by the weekend if nothing has improved or just let the batteries on the phones die out…..there’s nothing I can offer the project in terms of support now after spending hundreds if not nearly a couple of thousands on hardware and sims/routers including $20k on nodes, I do understand things take time, ofc they do, but those who invested on the back of a everything was ready to go live, partners invested, rewards good to go, were sold a lie tbh, that’s not fud, that’s facts, ok didn’t expect £96 off the bat, but if a license ain’t even making $15/20 a month minimum, who’s paying my bills?

As I didn’t get an answer, I would like to add this to #amaquestions 
—-
Dear @unityassistant 
Can you tell us whether we should wait for the clients to start paying for the tasks and get the ULOs only after that, or should we be trying to get as many ULOs as possible to make the network bigger and better? The problem with the latter one is that they won’t stick around  if there aren’t any rewards soon. author: Sophie | I will never DM you first
Eddie H
msg:
Don't believe I saw the same reply for the new SMS testing. Can you find it? 

I have not earned any UP from both caller ID or the new SMS testing. 
It sounds like each new task deployed needs time for the customer/enterprises to on board and increase volume. It sounds There are different time scales on different task types in relation to this. I.e. SMS sounds like the volume will increase faster than caller ID.

author: NEM0
msg: And you should believe it when it happens not before

author: Eddie H
msg:
To me it makes sense that there needs to be a bit of time for paying customers of unity to test the tasks and ensure they are happy with the service before deploying more resources into it. 
It also makes sense that each different task category Will be different on how this looks. 
I am choosing to believe what the team are stating in relation to the Wi-Fi task -Will be able to generate awards straight away. 
Obviously this is what I believe, you don't need to believe the same as me.

author: NEM0
msg: As far as unity is concerned I trust but verify. I trust this is their intention but will verify once the reports from you guys start coming in

author: Krabalot
msg: The messaging is the same as that for the CLI task. 'earning rewards from launch' is just stating the  capability. This capability is not quantified. I believe they are fully capable of projecting a task per day per license estimation based on discussions with the client. This would actually help in communicating expectations.
